description: An earthquake struck the region at 03:00 AM. The Structural Assessment Report on the Desktop (earthquake_assessment.txt) classifies each managed building by damage severity. Implement the emergency response in OpenMaint (login: admin/admin). For each Severe-damage building: create a Critical priority corrective maintenance work order titled 'EARTHQUAKE: Structural Assessment — [Building Name]' with the damage findings as description, and append '| EARTHQUAKE STATUS: RED — DO NOT OCCUPY' to the building's Name field. For each Moderate-damage building: create a High priority work order titled 'EARTHQUAKE: MEP Systems Inspection — [Building Name]' and append '| EARTHQUAKE STATUS: YELLOW — LIMITED OCCUPANCY' to the building's Name. For Minor-damage buildings: append '| EARTHQUAKE STATUS: GREEN — CLEARED' only — no work order. The report designates one building as the Emergency Operations Center. Create a new floor 'EOC — Temporary Operations' (code FLR-EOC-01) under that building, with three rooms: RM-EOC-CMD (EOC Command Room), RM-EOC-COM (EOC Communications Hub), and RM-EOC-SUP (EOC Supply Staging). A pre-existing work order for routine seismic sensor calibration exists in the system — do NOT modify or delete it or any other existing records.
